rulings.Case Title: MARIA A. NAVARRO et al VS. ADVOCATE MINES LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, July 22, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Hajoca Corporation¿s demurrer to first amended complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the first, second, third, fourth and ninth causes of action. As to the first through fourth causes of action, plaintiffs¿ failure to allege a specific product attributable to Hajoca requires the sustaining of the demurrer. Bockrath requires this specificity for product liability claims and California common law requires this specificity for a fraud claim. Moreover, as to the fourth cause of action, an independent basis for sustaining the demurrer is that plaintiffs failed to allege reliance on the alleged fraud. (Cadlo). While plaintiffs are not required to allege all of the Tarmann requirements for their fourth cause of action, reliance by decedent must still be alleged. Moreover, that cause of action, by combining disparate fraud theories is nearly incomprehensible and, for sake of clarity and notice to defendant, should be revised. Because the ninth cause of action is derivative of the first four causes of action, the demurrer is sustained to that cause of action as well. =(220/HEK)
